acceptance_criteria.name: Threat model covers STRIDE categories
acceptance_criteria.detail: STRIDE categories found in findings: ['Elevation of Privilege', 'Information Disclosure', 'Repudiation', 'Spoofing', 'Tampering']

acceptance_criteria.name: No critical/high vulnerabilities
acceptance_criteria.detail: FAILED — 6 critical/high findings: CORS-001, AUTH-001, PII-001, HDR-001, ENC-001, PHI-001

acceptance_criteria.name: PHI encryption at rest and in transit
acceptance_criteria.detail: FAILED — ENC-001 (SQLite not encrypted) and/or PHI-001 (raw PHI in logs) present

findings.title: Wildcard CORS policy allows any origin
findings.recommendation: Replace allow_origins=['*'] with an explicit allowlist of trusted origins (e.g. ['https://app.example.com']). Load from SAGE_ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var for environment parity.

findings.title: Authentication disabled by default (auth.enabled: false)
findings.recommendation: Set auth.enabled: true in production. Configure OIDC issuer_url, client_id, and client_secret. Add SAGE_AUTH_ENABLED=true to the deployment environment.

findings.title: PII/PHI detection disabled (pii.enabled: false)
findings.evidence: enabled: false            # set true to activate scrubbing
findings.recommendation: Set pii.enabled: true and install Presidio (pip install presidio-analyzer presidio-anonymizer). Consider setting fail_on_detection: true for HIPAA workloads to reject prompts containing raw PHI.

findings.title: Missing HTTP security headers: Strict-Transport-Security, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Content-Security-Policy
findings.evidence: Headers not found in api.py middleware chain
findings.recommendation: Add a SecurityHeadersMiddleware that sets: Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY, Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'.

findings.title: Unpinned dependency versions (27 packages)
findings.evidence: Unpinned: pyyaml, pydantic, langfuse, chromadb, langchain, langchain-community, langchain-chroma, langchain-huggingface...
findings.recommendation: Pin all versions: run `pip freeze > requirements-lock.txt` and use it in CI. Add `pip-audit` to the CI pipeline to detect known CVEs. Consider using `pip-compile` (pip-tools) to manage transitive deps.

findings.title: API keys hashed with SHA-256 but no salt (rainbow table risk)
findings.evidence: SHA-256 used without HMAC or salt in api_keys.py
findings.recommendation: Use HMAC-SHA256 with a secret key (hmac.new(SECRET.encode(), key.encode(), hashlib.sha256)) or bcrypt/argon2 for key storage. Add `secrets.token_bytes(32)` salt per key.

findings.title: Subprocess calls pass unsanitized environment to external CLI tools
findings.recommendation: Build the env dict from an allowlist of known-safe variables. Never pass os.environ directly. Validate CLI paths against an allowlist before execution.

findings.title: Audit log SQLite database not encrypted at application layer
findings.evidence: sqlite3 used without SQLCipher pragma key or field-level encryption
findings.recommendation: Use SQLCipher (pip install sqlcipher3) and set PRAGMA key='...' on connection open. Alternatively, encrypt the .sage/ directory with OS-level LUKS/BitLocker AND add a documented verification step to the deployment checklist. For PHI fields in audit records, apply Fernet field-level encryption.

findings.title: Audit log stores raw input_context / chat content — may contain PHI
findings.evidence: Fields 'input_context' and 'content' written to audit_log.db without redaction
findings.recommendation: Enable pii.enabled: true before writing to audit log. Run PII filter on input_context before persistence. For HIPAA: apply field-level encryption to content columns containing free text.

findings.title: Access audit logging is implemented (AuditLogger present, API wired)
findings.evidence: AuditLogger class and audit calls detected in audit_logger.py and api.py
findings.recommendation: Verify 100% endpoint coverage with an automated coverage check.

phase_1_recon: Identify unauthenticated endpoints when auth.enabled: false
phase_2_authentication: Test OIDC token validation with expired/malformed JWTs
Test API key bypass with invalid prefix formats
Fuzz the 'Approving as' field for RBAC bypass
phase_3_injection: Fuzz all string inputs for SQL injection (parameterized, low risk)
Test path traversal in file_name parameter (allowlist in place)
Test prompt injection via task.description → LLM CLI
Test environment variable injection via generic-cli provider
phase_4_authorization: Test RBAC bypass: submit yaml_edit without admin role
Test cross-tenant data access via X-SAGE-Tenant header manipulation
Verify proposal isolation: one tenant cannot approve another's proposals
phase_5_data_exposure: Inspect audit_log.db for plaintext PHI in input_context
Test for sensitive data leakage in API error messages
Check /gym/history and /gym/analytics for unredacted training data
Verify CORS: send requests from disallowed origins
phase_6_dos: Test rate limiting persistence across server restart
Flood /analyze and /build/start endpoints beyond rate limit
tools_recommended: sqlmap (SQL injection — expect clean results due to parameterization)
